Monthly Temperature Trends

Line Chart · 2010–2025
Clear seasonal cycle with Summer peaks above 75°F and Winter troughs in the low 30s. The shaded band shows wider daily variability in summer months.

Monthly Precipitation

Bar Chart
August is the wettest month driven by convective storms. February records the lowest precipitation.

Temperature Category Distribution

Donut Chart
Mild (31%) and Cool (26%) dominate — confirming a temperate maritime climate. Hot months represent only ~10% of records.

Temperature vs Precipitation

Scatter Plot
Weak positive trend — warmer months tend to be slightly wetter. Seasons cluster distinctly by temperature range.

Seasonal Temp by Decade

Grouped Bar Chart
Summer 2020s slightly warmer than 2010s, suggesting a marginal warming signal. Winter remains nearly identical across decades.

Temp Range by Season

Box Plot
Spring has the widest interquartile range — the most climatically unpredictable season. Summer is consistent but high.

📰 The Headline

Annapolis, Maryland experiences a temperate, seasonally predictable coastal climate — temperatures follow a highly regular annual cycle, precipitation is evenly spread, and the climate has remained remarkably stable over the 15-year study period (2010–2025). With an average temperature of 58.5°F and consistent year-round rainfall averaging 3.58 inches per month, this station reflects the broader humid subtropical conditions of the Chesapeake Bay region.

😲 What Surprised Us

Despite global warming narratives, the decadal comparison shows only marginal temperature differences between the 2010s and 2020s at this station. Local coastal moderation from the Chesapeake Bay appears to buffer broader warming trends. Additionally, Spring emerged as the most climatically "unpredictable" season — with the widest temperature range distribution — contrary to our expectation that Summer would show the most variability.

⚠️ Where to Pay Attention

The August precipitation spike combined with peak summer temperatures creates conditions favorable for storm-driven flooding. Emergency managers and municipal planners in the region should focus flood risk model updates on August–September data. The consistent wind patterns (averaging 6.8 mph) also have implications for marine traffic and coastal infrastructure planning in and around the Naval Academy.

✅ Decisions This Helps Make

  • Energy demand planning — seasonal temp cycles inform HVAC load forecasts
  • Agricultural scheduling — frost risk windows from TMIN trends
  • Tourism forecasting — Mild/Warm months (May–Sep) are peak season
  • Infrastructure risk — August flood prep based on precipitation patterns
  • Climate monitoring — decade-over-decade baseline for future anomaly detection

🛠 How Tableau Helped

Tableau's drag-and-drop interface enabled rapid iteration between chart types without writing code, accelerating insight discovery. Interactive filters (Season, Year, Temperature Category) allowed instant drill-down into sub-populations. Dashboard actions linked the scatter plot and bar chart so selecting a season in one view highlighted corresponding data in others. Tableau's automatic date parsing correctly identified YYYY-MM format and enabled seamless time-series exploration. Overall, Tableau transformed raw CSV data into a communicable, interactive story in a fraction of the time traditional reporting tools would require.
